mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rates compress equity discount rates and lower borrowing costs for corporations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate decline below 6.5 percent signals labor market tightening that boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55 percent of triggered instances over a three-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, supporting higher equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance (t-test p > 0.05) across rolling three-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals immediate supply-side weakness that triggers short-term equity sell-offs and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 40 percent of declining INDPRO instances during monetary tightening periods over a two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 2.5 percent trigger market repricing of future discount rates and force expectations of imminent policy tightening, generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of high-inflation observations over a three-year rolling evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market expansion, driving positive equity momentum over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between PAYEMS and UNRATE divergence and 20-day returns falls below 0.05 over rolling three-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth rates above 2.0 percent provide a persistent tailwind for corporate earnings, generating positive short-term equity returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0 percent over a two-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Weak industrial production alongside strong retail sales indicates a structural shift toward services and consumption, supporting higher equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: The INDPRO and RSAFS divergence signal produces negative 60-day returns in more than 65 percent of cases during structural transition periods over a three-year window.
